key_insight: Gradient clipping makes asynchronous SGD robust to worker stragglers by removing the oracle-complexity dependence on the maximum delay.
engineering_relevance: This suggests more stable and scalable distributed and federated training when some devices or workers are slow or intermittently delayed.
method: Model ASGD with constant step sizes under delayed updates from stragglers.
Use gradient clipping to bound the effect of stale gradients on optimization.
Analyze convergence under sub-Weibull gradient noise, covering heavy-tailed cases, and prove both expectation and high-probability guarantees.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence results, including the first high-probability guarantee for asynchronous optimization under this noise model.
watch_item: Clipping may improve robustness, but the clipping threshold and step size still need careful tuning to avoid slowing learning or biasing updates.
paper_title: Clipping Makes Distributed and Federated Asynchronous SGD Robust to Stragglers
